channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ¿A quién le avisas si los chatbots son usados para fines violentos?
item.description: Se intensifica el debate sobre la responsabilidad ética de los desarrolladores de reportar a los usuarios que buscan hacer daño apoyados por los chatbots.
item.dc:creator: Kashmir Hill

media:content.media:credit: Departamento de Policía Metropolitana de Las Vegas, vía Shutterstock
media:content.media:description: Matthew Livelsberger detonó material explosivo en un Cybertruck de Tesla frente al Trump International Hotel de Las Vegas en enero de 2025. Había consultado el plan con ChatGPT.

item.title: Un hueso hallado en España probaría que Aníbal usó elefantes de guerra
item.description: Según un grupo de arqueólogos, el espécimen de 2200 años ofrece la primera evidencia directa de cómo Cartago empleó a estos animales en las guerras púnicas.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz

media:content.media:description: Un fresco de la época renacentista atribuido a Jacopo Ripanda que representa a Aníbal en el lomo de un elefante durante la segunda guerra púnica, en el siglo III a. C.

item.title: Los melómanos jóvenes y nostálgicos redescubren el iPod
item.description: No es exactamente volver a lo analógico, pero la gente que quiere alejarse del “streaming” dice que el viejo dispositivo de Apple es un pequeño paso para salir de estar constantemente conectado.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 9, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘clandestine’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 178 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: clandestine
item.description: This word has appeared in 178 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Live Updates: Iran Names Khamenei’s Son Supreme Leader
item.description: Top clerics said Mojtaba Khamenei will succeed his slain father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The Israeli military said it was attacking Tehran and Beirut early Monday, and Iran said it had launched missiles toward Israel.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Stocks Tank in Asia as Oil Price Surges
item.description: Asian markets tumbled on Monday as Middle East tensions and spiking oil prices sparked a mass exit from the region’s previously booming stocks.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis and Meaghan Tobin

item.title: ‘Scarpetta,’ Plus 7 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new show staring Nicole Kidman premieres, and the 98th Academy Awards air live.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: Nicole Kidman and Simon Baker in “Scarpetta.”

item.title: How Trump Betrayed His First Duty to Americans
item.description: The Trump administration failed to plan for evacuating either its diplomats or its citizens and did not adequately warn either.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Feltman and Mara Karlin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Passengers evacuated from the Middle East gather at Dulles International Airport last week.

item.title: How Rising Home Insurance Costs Are Linked to Credit Scores
item.description: In many states, homeowners with excellent credit scores are charged less for home insurance than those with worse scores, even if they live in a disaster-prone area.
item.dc:creator: Claire Brown and Mira Rojanasakul

item.title: MrBeast ahora es un banquero
item.description: Beast Industries ha adquirido una aplicación similar a un banco para jóvenes y podría llegar a ofrecer diversos servicios financieros, incluidas las criptomonedas.
item.dc:creator: Tara Siegel Bernard and Ron Lieber

media:content.media:credit: Thea Traff para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jimmy Donaldson, de 27 años, más conocido como MrBeast, tiene muchos seguidores adolescentes y jóvenes. Ha dicho que quiere proporcionarles los recursos económicos básicos de los que él carecía cuando era más joven.

item.title: El desprecio de España a Trump me ha vuelto patriota
item.description: Los europeos, que tanto supimos matarnos en el pasado, estamos obligados a mostrar fortaleza moral ante los intentos de Trump de destruir el orden multilateral.
item.dc:creator: Paco Cerdà
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Fire Breaks Out Near Glasgow Central Station
item.description: Firefighters brought a blaze under control after it consumed a building on the same street as Scotland’s busiest station. It forced train service to close, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

media:content.media:description: More than 60 firefighters battled the blaze after it began at a shop near Glasgow’s main train station on Sunday.

item.title: On the Road With Zelensky, Weathered, Weary and Fighting On
item.description: Ukraine’s leader, Volodymyr Zelensky, traveled east to visit frontline troops trying to stave off Russian attacks, and invited reporters for The New York Times to go with him.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker and Brendan Hoffman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in Kramatorsk on Friday. “They need a lot of things,” he said of the soldiers. “And each day needs are changing.”

item.title: Ukraine Sent Drone Experts to Protect U.S. Bases in Jordan, Zelensky Says
item.description: As the war in Iran spreads, Kyiv is eagerly offering its hard-won expertise and advanced technology to counter Iranian drones.
item.dc:creator: Kim Barker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Soldiers in the Donetsk region of Ukraine in December, monitoring the sky to shoot down Russian Shahed drones using a 30-millimeter antiaircraft gun.

item.title: Is the Trial of Erdogan’s Top Rival More About Corruption or Politics?
item.description: Prosecutors accuse the former Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu of leading a criminal scheme. His supporters say Turkey’s president is trying to eliminate a political foe.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard, Gulsin Harman and Safak Timur

media:content.media:description: Demonstrators in Istanbul showing support for Ekrem Imamoglu, then the mayor of Istanbul, last March after he was arrested.

item.title: The Suburb That Won’t Sleep
item.description: Food is bringing thousands of people together to an unexpected place: a plaza west of Toronto. Some fear it’s tearing a neighborhood apart.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times

item.title: For China, Billions of Dollars Are at Risk From a Widening War
item.description: The country found a home in the Middle East for its investments and growing markets for steel, electric vehicles and solar panels. Those are now at stake.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Beilun Port in China. The Middle East became even more important to China after China’s trade rivalry with the United States escalated.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Met Opera’s Fervent Hunt for Funding
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, March 9, 2026.

item.title: Large Fire in Glasgow Forces Closure of Major Train Station
item.description: The blaze that started near Scotland’s busiest station was under control, but officials said they expected train service to be disrupted through Monday.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang

media:content.media:description: More than 60 firefighters battled the blaze after it began at a shop near Glasgow’s main train station on Sunday.

item.title: Democrats Seize on Oil Topping $100 a Barrel, as Trump Downplays Jump
item.description: The global oil benchmark topped $100 a barrel late Sunday, putting Republicans on the defensive on an issue at the center of this year’s midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump immediately sought to downplay the spike in oil prices.

item.title: U.S. Carries Out Another Boat Strike, Killing Six
item.description: The attack, in the eastern Pacific, was part of a continuing campaign by the U.S. Southern Command to target people suspected of smuggling drugs, and raised the overall death toll to at least 156.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )

item.title: Woman Shoots at Rihanna’s Mansion Near Beverly Hills
item.description: No injuries were reported and a woman about the age of 30 was taken into custody, the Los Angeles Police Department said.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales and Matt Stevens

media:content.media:credit: Nicolas Maeterlinck/Agence France-Presse, via Belga/Afp Via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: It is not clear if Rihanna, her partner, ASAP Rocky, or their children were home at the time of the shooting on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. Tomahawk Hit Naval Base Beside Iranian School, Video Shows
item.description: The evidence contradicts President Trump’s claim that Iran was responsible for a strike at the school that killed 175 people, most of them children.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: New Yorkers Embrace the First Warm Day of the Season
item.description: After a frigid, snowy, seemingly interminable winter, the first taste of mild air had people shedding layers and flocking to parks.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall

media:content.media:description: Washington Square Park on Sunday, as the temperature rose above 60 degrees.

item.title: Los BRICS están divididos respecto a Irán
item.description: Brasil, China y Rusia denunciaron los ataques de EE. UU. e Israel, pero otros países del grupo BRICS no lo han hecho, a pesar de que Irán es uno de sus miembros.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El primer ministro de China, Li Qiang, el primer ministro de India, Narendra Modi, el presidente de Brasil, Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, y el presidente de Sudáfrica, Cyril Ramaphosa, en una cumbre de los BRICS celebrada en Río de Janeiro el año pasado.

item.title: Pain of Combat Deaths Doesn’t Fade Over Time, Families Say
item.description: The deaths of American troops in the Iran war are resurfacing the hurt felt by families of those lost in previous wars. “I know what they’re feeling — their whole world is falling apart,” one woman said.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A memorial service for the fallen U.S. Marine Kareem Grant Nikoui in September 2021.

item.title: Iran Chooses New Supreme Leader
item.description: Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in recent U.S.-Israeli strikes, as its next supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang and McKinnon de Kuyper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Middle East Images, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, the son of the Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in 2019.

item.title: Georgia Teacher Is Killed After Teenagers’ Prank Goes Wrong
item.description: The teacher’s family “supports getting the charges dropped for all involved,” after a planned toilet-paper prank became a fatal car accident.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

item.title: White House Removes Republican Member of N.T.S.B.
item.description: J. Todd Inman, who was prominent in the investigation of the midair collision in Washington last year, said no reason was given for his firing two years into his term on the transportation safety board.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: J. Todd Inman of the National Transportation Safety Board during a Jan. 27 hearing on the midair collision that killed 67 people near Reagan National Airport the previous January.

item.title: Iran’s Security Establishment Celebrates Mojtaba Khamenei’s Selection
item.description: Opponents of the government, however, worried the new supreme leader, a son of the recently killed Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, would rule with an iron fist like his father.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A poster of Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei in Tehran on the night he was announced as the new supreme leader of Iran.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the Conflict on Sunday
item.description: Iran named Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as his father’s successor. The Pentagon announced the death of a seventh U.S. service member.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Smoke plumes rising from an oil storage site in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Sunday.

item.title: Pentagon Announces Death of Seventh U.S. Service Member in War With Iran
item.description: Another American service member died in the war with Iran, the Pentagon said on Sunday, bringing the number of American troops killed in the conflict to seven. The service member died after being seriously injured on March 1, when Iran struck a Saudi military base where American troops were stationed, U.S. Central Command said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The first six American service members killed in the war with Iran were returned to Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Saturday.

item.title: Oil Prices Spike Over $110 a Barrel, Highest Since Pandemic
item.description: The jump was a sign of growing concern that the war in the Middle East will take a toll on energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The price of a gallon of regular gasoline has climbed about 16 percent since the United States and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28.

item.title: Who Is Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s New Supreme Leader?
item.description: The selection of a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in a strike on his compound at the start of the war, could anger Iranians seeking change.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, Sanam Mahoozi and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Office of The Iranian Supreme Leader/Wana News Agency, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A handout image from Iran state media of Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 9, 2026
item.description: Christina Iverson and Andrea Carla Michaels team up for a fourth collaboration.
item.dc:creator: Ikechukwu Ufomadu

media:content.media:description: You like this puzzle. You really, really like it.

item.title: Iran Has a New Supreme Leader. What Does That Mean?
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei takes on a role that makes him not only Iran’s spiritual leader but also the highest authority in the land.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, at a rally in Tehran in 2019.

item.title: U.S. Authorities Are Investigating Device Thrown Near Gracie Mansion
item.description: The device that emitted smoke during a protest near Gracie Mansion Saturday was designed to be deadly, three people with knowledge of the investigation said.
item.dc:creator: William K. Rashbaum, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Maria Cramer

media:content.media:description: Emir Balat runs as police officers approach and a homemade bomb starts to emit smoke outside Gracie Mansion on Saturday.

item.title: Sizing Up Iran’s Elite Guards
item.description: Many see the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps as the main barrier to regime change, or any change at all, in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei, Son of Iran’s Slain Supreme Leader, Is a Mysterious Figure
item.description: The succession of the slain leader’s son is seen as a signal of the Islamic republic’s defiance of Israel and the United States, and of continuity during crisis.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Middle East Images, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, the son of the Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in 2019.

item.title: State Dept. Said to Order Diplomats in Saudi Arabia to Leave
item.description: The ordered departure of U.S. employees in the kingdom indicates that senior diplomats are bracing for a possible surge in violence in the war with Iran, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Mark Mazzetti, Vivian Nereim and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The U.S. Embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

item.title: René Redzepi, chef de Noma y las denuncias de abusos que lo aquejan
item.description: Para algunos exempleados de Noma, el chef René Redzepi no ha reconocido el alcance de la violencia que, según ellos, infligió durante años.
item.dc:creator: Julia Moskin

media:content.media:credit: John McConnico para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Noma, en Copenhague, se ha convertido en uno de los restaurantes más famosos del mundo bajo la dirección del chef René Redzepi. Extrabajadores han denunciado que la reputación del lugar se construyó sobre condiciones laborales abusivas.

item.title: Have a Montana License Plate in California? Officials Have an Eye on You.
item.description: Since 2018, Californians have bought luxury vehicles worth over $20 million in sales-tax-free Montana, exploiting a loophole that avoided millions of dollars in levies, an investigation revealed.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan

media:content.media:credit: Office of the Attorney General of California
media:content.media:description: The California Attorney General’s Office said that 14 people arranged tax-free sales of vehicles, including this $1.5 million Porsche 918 Spyder, by making the purchases in Montana.

item.title: Pentagon Announces Seventh U.S. Death in War With Iran
item.description: The service member killed was not publicly identified, but U.S. Central Command said the death was caused by injuries after an attack on a Saudi military base.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The first six American service members killed in the war with Iran were returned to Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Saturday.

item.title: A Beirut Hotel Sheltering the Displaced Comes Under Fire
item.description: A Christian hotel owner opened her doors to Shia Muslims fleeing Israel’s bombardment of the southern outskirts of Beirut. Then a missile struck the hotel, killing her 34-year-old receptionist.
item.dc:creator: Simona Foltyn, Adrian Hartrick, Ang Li, Mark Boyer, Santiago García Muñoz and Jon Hazell

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: Energy Secretary Says Tankers Could Start Moving Soon
item.description: “We’re not too long, I think, before you will see more regular resumption of ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz,” Chris Wright said on CNN on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Tankers anchored in Muscat, Oman. Chris Wright, the energy secretary, said it would take “a few weeks” at most for normal numbers of tankers to return to the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Park Service Warns of Quicksand at Glen Canyon National Recreation
item.description: The service issued the alert for the Glen Canyon National Recreation Area, which covers more than 1.25 million acres across Arizona and Utah.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz
item.category: Glen Canyon National Recreation Area (Ariz and Utah)

media:content.media:description: The Colorado River near the Glen Canyon National Recreation Area in Arizona in July. The National Park Service warns visitors to be on the lookout for quicksand.

item.title: U.S. Continues Airstrikes Against a Wide Array of Targets in Iran
item.description: A senior U.S. military official said those targets included missile launchers and air-defense sites.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Eric Schmitt and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Smoke rising in Tehran on Sunday after airstrikes.

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
